message:
perf(storage): make ready/list sorts deterministic with id tiebreaker

`sort_ready_hybrid` and `sort_list_default` previously left ties
unbroken when two issues had the same priority bucket and creation
timestamp. With the existing comparators that meant:

- `ready` output drifted between calls when the underlying source
  re-fetched in a different order (cargo test was flaky on parallel
  runs because of this; same input issues but different "first" issue
  in the listing).
- `list_issues` default ordering exposed the same flakiness whenever
  the SQL layer returned ties (which is most issue sets — priority is
  one of P0..P4 and bulk-created issues frequently share a second-
  truncated `created_at`).

Two changes here:

1. Append `.then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))` as the final
   tiebreaker. Issue IDs are unique and lexically stable, so the
   resulting order is fully deterministic regardless of input order.
2. Switch `sort_by` to `sort_unstable_by`. With a fully total ordering
   we don't need stability — two equal elements would have to share
   the same id, which is impossible. `sort_unstable_by` is faster
   (typically pdqsort-based, no allocation, no stability bookkeeping)
   and avoids needlessly retaining the source order, which would have
   masked rather than fixed the flakiness above.

No public API change; only the relative order of ties is affected,
and only in the direction of "now deterministic instead of
input-order-dependent."
